methods.description: This method involves constructing phylogenetic trees using maximum likelihood and Bayesian inference to analyze evolutionary relationships among species.

steps.description: Maximum likelihood (ML) trees were constructed in raxml-GUI v1.5b2, with 1000 bootstrap replicates in a rapid bootstrap analysis, using GTR+G+I substitution model with partitions.
steps.searchable_summary: construct ML trees using raxml-GUI with 1000 bootstrap replicates

steps.description: Bayesian inference analysis (BI) was performed in two parallel runs in MrBayes, consisting each of four chains of six million generations with a sampling frequency 1000 generation for one tree and burn-in of 25%.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Bayesian inference analysis using MrBayes with parallel runs

steps.description: Partitions were in PartionFinder using greedy search and selected according to aicc.
steps.searchable_summary: select partitions using PartionFinder with greedy search

steps.description: The convergence was checked in Tracer v1.7 and terminated when ESS were superior to 200 with the initial 25% trees as burn-in.
steps.searchable_summary: check convergence using Tracer and terminate when ESS > 200

methods.description: This method involves the collection and morphological analysis of specimens, including mounting, measurement, and digital photography.

steps.description: The examined specimens were mounted on slides following the procedure by Saether (1969).
steps.searchable_summary: mount specimens on slides using Saether's procedure

steps.description: Measurements are given as ranges followed by the mean when there are four or more specimens examined.
steps.searchable_summary: measure specimens and calculate ranges and means

steps.description: Digital photographs were captured with a Leica DFC420 camera using a Leica DM6000 B compound microscope and under the application of the software Leica Suite.
steps.searchable_summary: capture digital photographs using Leica DFC420 and DM6000 B

steps.description: Photograph postprocessing were done in Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator.
steps.searchable_summary: postprocess photographs using Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator
